message.content:
POST-MORTEM. What went wrong (measured, on the real 329-basket corpus): S2/S3 produced a cp3 corpus
where only 3/995 evidence rows had a DOI and 3/995 had a journal name, and 253/995 were tier=UNKNOWN
— even though the metadata was PLAINLY PRESENT in each row's own direct_quote text and source_url
(e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo's row literally contains "Journal of Economic Perspectives" and DOI
10.1257/jep.33.2.3, yet its doi/journal fields were blank). A trivial downstream deterministic pass
(regex DOI + domain->venue map) recovered doi 3->240, journal 3->294, and cut UNKNOWN 253->145.
So S2/S3 SILENTLY DROPPED metadata it had in hand. Consequences: good peer-reviewed sources got
mislabeled UNKNOWN (looks low-quality), instruction-following on journal-demanding tasks tanked, and
nothing anywhere FAILED or warned — the corpus looked fine.

READ-ONLY: read the S2/S3 code but DO NOT WRITE to /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (a fix wheel is editing it).
Read from /workspace/POLARIS/src (or the s2s3 worktree) read-only; write any findings to
/home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md ONLY. The proven recovery logic is at
/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py. Corpus: cp3_basket_snapshot.json
(995 rows). Be concrete: file:line for the failure. Honest — if the extractor was never written vs
written-but-never-called vs silently-excepted, say which.


Given the root cause:
{
  "mechanism": "The DOI/journal/tier extractor for the S2/S3 path was NEVER WRITTEN. S2 (scripts/s2_select_replay.py HEAD) builds the cp2 kept-row pool with zero metadata enrichment: pass-through rows are appended raw (line 252 `kept_rows.append(row)`) and screened rows go through `ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res)` (line 268), which is defined in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386 and only rewrites the body field + attaches a line_screen sidecar — it does `dict(row)` (preserving any doi/journal already present) but never parses direct_quote/source_url to derive doi/journal/tier. There is no import of any extractor at HEAD. S3 (scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py HEAD) then only reads the fields S2 never filled: `_tier(i)` at line 101-102 returns `str(rows[i].get('tier','') or 'UNKNOWN')` — a wrong-default that silently coerces every blank tier to 'UNKNOWN' (that single `or 'UNKNOWN'` is the 253 UNKNOWN) — and there is no `_doi`/`_journal` accessor and no member_dois/member_journals at HEAD at all, so DOI/journal were never even surfaced. The DOI in source_url (e.g. .../doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3) and the 'Journal of Economic Perspectives' string in direct_quote were in hand on every row and never read. The ~5 rows that arrived pre-populated upstream (acemoglu) flowed through intact, proving it is absence-of-extraction, not a transform drop.",
  "file_line": "scripts/s2_select_replay.py:248-268 (no enrichment; apply_result_to_row at src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386) and scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:101-102 (_tier ... or 'UNKNOWN'; no _doi/_journal)",
  "failure_class": "never_written",
  "mechanism_note": "n/a",
  "was_silent": true,
  "evidence": "Confirmed against committed HEAD of /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (working tree is mid-fix by the wheel: it adds untracked src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py + enrich_row_metadata calls — the fix, not the bug). HEAD s2_select_replay.py has no metadata extractor import and no doi/journal/tier writes; apply_result_to_row (line_screen.py:1361-1386) touches only body+sidecar. HEAD s3_consolidate_replay.py grep shows only `def _tier` (101) and `member_tiers_weight` (177) — no _doi/_journal/member_dois. The shipped outputs prove silence: outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json has no metadata_enrichment_summary; outputs/s2s3_repass/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json has no corpus_metadata_summary and baskets lack member_dois. Measured on that cp2 (728 rows): only 5 doi, only 5 journal, 177 UNKNOWN; 723 blank-doi rows, 156 with a DOI directly regex-recoverable from their own source_url/direct_quote (21 from url, 135 from quote); blank rows (e.g. ev_454) don't even carry a doi/journal key. Nothing raised or warned; stage summaries never measured population. Full write-up: /home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md",
  "other_silent_losses": [
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:102 _tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN' — wrong-default silently laundering any missing tier into UNKNOWN",
    "scripts/s2_select_replay.py ~line 221 topic-judge except Exception (fail-open) — any judge error silently yields zero off-subject stamps",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 142 _stmt bare except around extract_numeric_claims — swallows extraction errors, silent fallback to weaker representative",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 63 _nli_score_stats except Exception — semantic-merge telemetry can silently become unavailable",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 351 unified_deletion_disclosure except Exception — swallows S2 summary.json read/parse errors",
    "Row schema by omission: blank rows (e.g. ev_454) carry no doi/journal key at all; every .get('doi','') consumer sees empty and no S2/S3 code backfills, so the gap is invisible without a population lock-bar that HEAD never had"
  ]
}
Design the PREVENTION so this class of silent metadata-loss can NEVER recur on ANY query. It must be:
(1) a FAIL-LOUD INVARIANT enforced on every S2/S3 run — e.g. after consolidation, assert that any row
whose direct_quote/url contains a DOI pattern has doi populated, and any row from a known-journal
domain is not tier=UNKNOWN; if violated, FAIL LOUD with the offending counts (not a silent pass).
(2) QUERY-AGNOSTIC — it must hold for a finance query, a medical query, anything, not just AI-labor.
Use structural signals (DOI regex, domain class), never topic keywords.
(3) a REGRESSION TEST that reproduces this exact failure and would have caught it, and keeps catching
it. State WHERE the invariant runs so it fires on every future corpus build. Note false-positive risk
(e.g. a genuine non-journal source correctly having no DOI must not trip it).

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Repair corpus metadata: populate DOI + journal/publisher + re-tier from signals we ALREADY have
3	(the direct_quote text, the source_url, the title). Deterministic, conservative, non-destructive.
4	
5	The bug this fixes: 992/995 rows had empty doi/journal and 253 were tier=UNKNOWN, even though the
6	source is clearly a peer-reviewed journal (e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo JEP, DOI in the text). Good sources
7	were being mislabeled, which (a) makes the corpus look low-quality and (b) tanks instruction-following
8	on benchmarks that demand journal sources. We recover the metadata instead of re-fetching.
9	
10	Rule of law (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): weight-and-consolidate, never filter. This only RE-LABELS tier and
11	FILLS missing metadata from evidence already present; it deletes nothing.
12	"""
13	import json, re, sys, collections, copy
14	
15	SRC = "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json"
16	OUT = "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.repaired.json"
17	
18	DOI_RE = re.compile(r'10\.\d{4,9}/[-._;()/:A-Za-z0-9]+')
19	
20	# Defensible domain -> (tier, publisher/venue-kind). Tiers per the project's T1(top primary) .. T7 scale.
21	# T1-T2 = peer-reviewed journals / top working-paper series; T3-T4 = gov/central-bank/IGO/institutional;
22	# T5 = industry/consulting; T6-T7 = tertiary/blog/marketing. Only assign on a CLEAR domain signal.
23	DOMAIN_TIER = {
24	    # peer-reviewed journals & top academic (T1)
25	    'aeaweb.org':'T1','sciencedirect.com':'T1','link.springer.com':'T1','cambridge.org':'T1',
26	    'emerald.com':'T1','nber.org':'T1','onlinelibrary.wiley.com':'T1','tandfonline.com':'T1',
27	    'journals.sagepub.com':'T1','academic.oup.com':'T1','mdpi.com':'T2','arxiv.org':'T2',
28	    'papers.ssrn.com':'T2','ideas.repec.org':'T2','econstor.eu':'T2','cepr.org':'T2',
29	    'pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov':'T1','pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov':'T1','revistes.ub.edu':'T1',
30	    'econbiz.de':'T2','siepr.stanford.edu':'T2','economics.mit.edu':'T2','nature.com':'T1',
31	    # gov / central bank / IGO / institutional (T3-T4)
32	    'bls.gov':'T3','congress.gov':'T3','oecd.org':'T3','imf.org':'T3','ilo.org':'T3',
33	    'openknowledge.worldbank.org':'T3','newyorkfed.org':'T3','federalreserve.gov':'T3',
34	    'brookings.edu':'T4','reports.weforum.org':'T4','weforum.org':'T4','whitehouse.gov':'T3',
35	    # industry / consulting (T5)
36	    'goldmansachs.com':'T5','mckinsey.com':'T5','hbsp.harvard.edu':'T5','shrm-res.cloudinary.com':'T5',
37	    # tertiary / blog / marketing (T6-T7)
38	    'en.wikipedia.org':'T7','static1.squarespace.com':'T6','ssir.org':'T5','medium.com':'T7',
39	}
40	# publisher/venue label by domain (for the journal field when the content doesn't name it)
41	DOMAIN_VENUE = {
42	    'aeaweb.org':'American Economic Association','nber.org':'NBER Working Paper',
43	    'papers.ssrn.com':'SSRN','arxiv.org':'arXiv','ideas.repec.org':'RePEc','econstor.eu':'EconStor',
44	    'cepr.org':'CEPR','congress.gov':'Congressional Research Service','oecd.org':'OECD',
45	    'imf.org':'IMF','ilo.org':'ILO','bls.gov':'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics',
46	    'openknowledge.worldbank.org':'World Bank','newyorkfed.org':'Federal Reserve Bank of New York',
47	    'reports.weforum.org':'World Economic Forum','weforum.org':'World Economic Forum',
48	    'pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov':'PubMed Central','brookings.edu':'Brookings Institution',
49	}
50	# journal names commonly present in econ/AI-labor text -> use if found in the quote
51	JOURNAL_PAT = re.compile(r'(Journal of [A-Z][A-Za-z ]+|Review of [A-Z][A-Za-z ]+|Quarterly Journal of [A-Za-z ]+|'
52	                         r'American Economic Review|Econometrica|Research Policy|Labour Economics|'
53	                         r'Technological Forecasting and Social Change|Nature[A-Za-z ]*)')
54	
55	def dom_of(url):
56	    p = url.split('/')
57	    return p[2].replace('www.','') if len(p) > 2 and url.startswith('http') else ''
58	
59	def repair_row(r, stats):
60	    url = str(r.get('source_url') or r.get('url') or '')
61	    quote = str(r.get('direct_quote','')); title = str(r.get('title',''))
62	    dom = dom_of(url)
63	    # 1) DOI
64	    if not str(r.get('doi','')).strip():
65	        m = DOI_RE.search(quote) or DOI_RE.search(url)
66	        if m: r['doi'] = m.group(0).rstrip('.'); stats['doi_filled'] += 1
67	    # 2) journal/venue
68	    if not str(r.get('journal','')).strip():
69	        jm = JOURNAL_PAT.search(quote) or JOURNAL_PAT.search(title)
70	        if jm: r['journal'] = jm.group(1).strip(); stats['journal_filled'] += 1
71	        elif dom in DOMAIN_VENUE: r['journal'] = DOMAIN_VENUE[dom]; stats['journal_filled'] += 1
72	    # 3) tier — only upgrade from UNKNOWN/empty on a CLEAR signal; never downgrade a real tier
73	    cur = str(r.get('tier') or '').strip()
74	    if cur in ('', 'UNKNOWN'):
75	        new = None
76	        if str(r.get('doi','')).strip(): new = DOMAIN_TIER.get(dom, 'T2')  # has DOI => at least a paper
77	        elif dom in DOMAIN_TIER: new = DOMAIN_TIER[dom]
78	        if new:
79	            r['tier'] = new; r['tier_repaired'] = True; stats['tier_reclassed'] += 1
80	            stats['tier_to'][new] += 1
81	    return r
82	
83	def main():
84	    d = json.load(open(SRC))
85	    rows = []
86	    def walk(o):
87	        if isinstance(o, dict):
88	            if o.get('direct_quote') is not None and (o.get('source_url') or o.get('url')): rows.append(o)
89	            for v in o.values(): walk(v)
90	        elif isinstance(o, list):
91	            for x in o: walk(x)
92	    walk(d)
93	
94	    before = collections.Counter(str(r.get('tier') or 'UNKNOWN') for r in rows)
95	    stats = {'doi_filled':0,'journal_filled':0,'tier_reclassed':0,'tier_to':collections.Counter()}
96	    for r in rows: repair_row(r, stats)
97	    after = collections.Counter(str(r.get('tier') or 'UNKNOWN') for r in rows)
98	
99	    # dedup detection (report only — do NOT delete, per §-1.3)
100	    key = collections.Counter()
101	    for r in rows:
102	        k = (str(r.get('doi','')).strip() or str(r.get('source_url') or r.get('url') or '') or str(r.get('title',''))).lower()
103	        key[k] += 1
104	    dups = sum(c-1 for c in key.values() if c > 1)
105	
106	    json.dump(d, open(OUT,'w'), indent=1)
107	    print("=== METADATA REPAIR ===")
108	    print(f"rows: {len(rows)}")
109	    print(f"DOI filled:        {stats['doi_filled']}")
110	    print(f"journal filled:    {stats['journal_filled']}")
111	    print(f"tier reclassified: {stats['tier_reclassed']}  -> {dict(stats['tier_to'])}")
112	    print(f"\nUNKNOWN before: {before.get('UNKNOWN',0)}   after: {after.get('UNKNOWN',0)}")
113	    print(f"tier dist AFTER: {dict(sorted(after.items()))}")
114	    print(f"\nduplicate rows detected (same doi/url/title): {dups} (reported, NOT deleted)")
115	    print(f"\nwrote repaired corpus -> {OUT}")
116	
117	if __name__ == '__main__':
118	    main()
119

1	# S2/S3 metadata-loss post-mortem (root cause)
2	
3	**Verdict: the DOI/journal/tier extractor for the S2/S3 path was NEVER WRITTEN.**
4	Not written-but-uncalled, not silently-excepted, not populated-then-dropped. The S2 SELECT+WEIGH
5	stage and the S3 CONSOLIDATE stage carried each row's `direct_quote` + `source_url` (which contain
6	the DOI and the journal name) end-to-end but never parsed them into the `doi` / `journal` / `tier`
7	fields. Rows entered with those fields blank and left with them blank. Completely silent — nothing
8	failed, nothing warned, and the stage summaries did not even *measure* DOI/journal/tier population.
9	
10	All line numbers below are the **committed HEAD** of `/home/polaris/wt/s2s3` (the bug state). The
11	working tree is currently being edited by the fix wheel (it adds `enrich_row_metadata` +
12	`src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py`, which are untracked/new), so read HEAD to see the
13	defect, not the working tree.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## The mechanism, at file:line
18	
19	### S2 — `scripts/s2_select_replay.py` (HEAD)
20	S2 builds the cp2 kept-row pool in one loop and **applies no metadata enrichment at all**:
21	
22	- Line 248–252 — pass-through rows (`res is None`) are appended RAW:
23	  ```py
24	  for row in rows:
25	      ...
26	      if res is None:
27	          kept_rows.append(row)          # raw row, no enrichment
28	  ```
29	- Line 268 — screened rows get only a body rewrite + sidecar:
30	  ```py
31	  kept_rows.append(ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res))
32	  ```
33	- There is **no import of any metadata extractor** at HEAD (no `source_metadata`, no
34	  `enrich_row_metadata`). `apply_result_to_row` is defined in
35	  `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386` and does exactly two things: rewrites the
36	  widest body field to the kept lines and attaches `new_row["line_screen"] = result.sidecar()`. It
37	  copies the row with `dict(row)`, so it **preserves** any doi/journal already present but **never
38	  derives** them. It touches doi/journal/tier nowhere.
39	
40	Net: S2 was purely a body screener. The DOI sitting in `source_url`
41	(`.../doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3`) and the "Journal of Economic Perspectives" string in `direct_quote`
42	were in hand on every row and never read.
43	
44	### S3 — `scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py` (HEAD)
45	S3 only *reads back* the fields S2 never populated, and hard-codes the wrong default:
46	
47	- Line 101–102 — the tier accessor:
48	  ```py
49	  def _tier(i):
50	      return (str(rows[i].get('tier', '') or 'UNKNOWN')) if 0 <= i < len(rows) else 'UNKNOWN'
51	  ```
52	  This is a **wrong-default**: any row whose `tier` is blank/missing is silently coerced to
53	  `'UNKNOWN'`, even when the domain (`aeaweb.org`, `tandfonline.com`, `nber.org`, …) makes the tier
54	  trivially derivable. That single `or 'UNKNOWN'` is why 253/995 read UNKNOWN.
55	- There is **no `_doi` and no `_journal` accessor at HEAD, and no `member_dois` / `member_journals`
56	  in the basket dict** — DOI and journal were never even surfaced to the basket level. S3 could not
57	  have propagated metadata it never looked at.
58	
59	So the two stages compose into: never-extract (S2) → read-blank-and-default-to-UNKNOWN (S3).
60	
61	---
62	
63	## Why it was totally silent
64	- HEAD S2 `summary.json` and HEAD S3 `consolidation_summary` had **no DOI/journal/tier lock-bar** —
65	  no `metadata_enrichment_summary`, no `corpus_metadata_summary`. (Confirmed: the shipped
66	  `outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json` has `metadata_enrichment_summary` absent, and
67	  `outputs/s2s3_repass/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json` has no `corpus_metadata_summary` and baskets
68	  with no `member_dois`.) The stages never asserted anything about metadata, so blank fields were
69	  indistinguishable from a healthy run.
70	- Nothing raised or warned. There is no code path that even *attempts* extraction, so there was no
71	  exception to swallow.
72	
73	## Not "dropped in transform"
74	The ~5 rows that *did* arrive with metadata upstream (e.g. `acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks`
75	carries `doi=10.1257/jep.33.2.3`, `journal=Journal of Economic Perspectives`, `tier=T1`) flowed
76	through S2/S3 **intact** — `dict(row)` in `apply_result_to_row` preserves them. So the loss is not a
77	transform/serialization drop of populated fields; it is a total absence of population for the
78	~99.5% of rows that entered blank. Measured on the cp2 that fed the reported cp3:
79	728 rows, only 5 with `doi`, only 5 with `journal`, 177 `UNKNOWN`; **723 blank-doi rows, of which
80	156 have a DOI directly regex-recoverable from their own `source_url`/`direct_quote`** and far more
81	have a domain-derivable journal — exactly what the downstream repair pass recovered.
82	
83	## Proof the data was in hand
84	`repair_corpus_metadata.py` (the proven recovery) does nothing S2/S3 couldn't have done inline: one
85	DOI regex over `direct_quote`+`source_url`, a domain→venue map, a journal-name regex over the quote,
86	and a domain→tier map. It lifted doi 3→240, journal 3→294, UNKNOWN 253→145. The inputs it used
87	(`direct_quote`, `source_url`, `title`, domain) are the same fields S2 already had on every row at
88	line 253 and S3 had at every `rows[i]` accessor. Nothing needed re-fetching.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## Other spots in S2/S3 that can silently drop data the same way
93	1. **`scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:102` — `_tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN'`** — the wrong-default itself;
94	   any future missing tier is silently laundered into UNKNOWN rather than surfaced.
95	2. **`scripts/s2_select_replay.py` topic-judge `except Exception` (fail-open, ~line 221 working
96	   tree / HEAD equivalent)** — on any judge error it prints and continues with zero off-subject
97	   stamps; a real defect degrades silently to "kept everything, stamped nothing".
98	3. **`scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py` `_stmt` bare `except Exception` around
99	   `extract_numeric_claims` (~line 142)** — swallows extraction failures and silently falls back to
100	   a weaker representative sentence with no disclosure.
101	4. **`scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py` `_nli_score_stats` `except Exception` (~line 63)** — returns
102	   `{'available': False}`; the semantic-merge telemetry can silently vanish.
103	5. **`scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py` unified-deletion-disclosure `except Exception` (~line 351)** —
104	   swallows S2 `summary.json` read/parse errors into an `{'error': ...}` blob rather than failing.
105	6. **Row schema by omission** — blank rows do not even carry a `doi`/`journal` key (e.g. `ev_454`
106	   has neither key, only `tier`). Every consumer using `.get('doi','')` sees empty and no S2/S3 code
107	   backfills, so the gap is invisible unless someone measures population — which HEAD never did.
108	
109	## Fix direction (already being applied by the wheel, for reference)
110	Run the deterministic extractor at the S2 stamp site so cp2 carries correct doi/journal/tier, and
111	add a DOI/journal/tier population lock-bar to both stage summaries so a blank corpus fails loud
112	instead of looking fine. The working tree already wires `enrich_row_metadata`
113	(`src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py`) into `s2_select_replay.py:248-257` and adds
114	`member_dois`/`member_journals` + `corpus_metadata_summary` to `s3_consolidate_replay.py`.

# PREVENTION — a fail-loud, query-agnostic metadata invariant

## The gap that still remains after the wheel's fix
The wheel restored the extractor and added lock-bar **measurements**
(`metadata_enrichment_summary` in `s2_select_replay.py:275-299`, `corpus_metadata_summary` in
`s3_consolidate_replay.py:223-251`). But those blocks only **count and print** — grep for
`assert`/`raise`/`FAIL` around them returns nothing. A measurement is not a guard: a run that
silently reverts the extractor (import removed, call skipped, a refactor that drops the
pass-through branch) still writes a summary and passes. Worse, you **cannot** turn the counts into
a guard with a threshold, because population is query-dependent: a legitimate finance/policy corpus
can correctly have very few DOIs. A threshold on `doi_populated` would false-fire on honest
non-journal corpora and still miss a partial regression. The guard must be **per-row structural**,
not aggregate.

## The invariant (structural residual, recomputed independently)
Re-derive the structural signal from each row's OWN `direct_quote`/`source_url` using the SAME
detector the extractor uses, then assert the extracted field is populated. Two rules, both
query-agnostic (DOI regex + domain class only — zero topic keywords):

- **R1 (DOI residual = 0):** if `DOI_RE.search(direct_quote OR source_url)` matches, then `doi`
  MUST be non-empty. A match with an empty `doi` means enrichment never ran on that row.
- **R2 (known-journal-domain tier residual = 0):** if `domain_of(url)` is in `DOMAIN_TIER`
  (the known scholarly/gov/venue set), then `tier` MUST NOT be `UNKNOWN`/blank.

Because the invariant recomputes with the **exact same `DOI_RE` / `DOMAIN_TIER`** the extractor
uses, extractor and check can never disagree by construction: a non-zero residual is a proof that
`enrich_row_metadata` did not run (or was bypassed) on those rows — i.e. it reproduces the HEAD
bug's signature directly. It FAILS LOUD with the offending counts and a few example evidence_ids.

```python
# src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py  (add next to enrich_rows)
def assert_metadata_invariants(rows, *, stage: str) -> dict:
    """FAIL LOUD if any row structurally carries a DOI but has doi blank, or sits on a known
    journal/venue domain yet is tier=UNKNOWN. Query-agnostic: DOI regex + domain class only.
    Raises SystemExit (non-zero) with offending counts + example ids. Returns the audit dict on pass."""
    doi_viol, tier_viol = [], []
    for r in rows:
        if not isinstance(r, dict):
            continue
        url = str(r.get('source_url') or r.get('url') or '')
        quote = str(r.get('direct_quote', '') or '')
        if extract_doi(quote, url) and not str(r.get('doi', '') or '').strip():
            doi_viol.append(str(r.get('evidence_id', '')))
        if domain_of(url) in DOMAIN_TIER and str(r.get('tier') or 'UNKNOWN') == 'UNKNOWN':
            tier_viol.append(str(r.get('evidence_id', '')))
    audit = {'stage': stage, 'n_rows': len(rows),
             'doi_residual_violations': len(doi_viol), 'tier_residual_violations': len(tier_viol)}
    if doi_viol or tier_viol:
        raise SystemExit(
            f"[{stage}] METADATA INVARIANT VIOLATED — extractor did not run on all rows.\n"
            f"  rows with a DOI in their own quote/url but doi BLANK: {len(doi_viol)} "
            f"(e.g. {doi_viol[:8]})\n"
            f"  rows on a known-journal domain but tier=UNKNOWN:      {len(tier_viol)} "
            f"(e.g. {tier_viol[:8]})\n"
            f"  This is the S2/S3 silent-metadata-loss class. Enrich before banking the corpus.")
    return audit
```

## WHERE it runs — so it fires on EVERY future corpus build
Call it at **both** stage boundaries, on the exact pool that gets banked, guaranteeing coverage no
matter which stage a future regression lands in:

1. **S2 — `scripts/s2_select_replay.py`**, immediately after the lock-bar block (right after
   line 293, before `cp2["evidence_for_gen"] = kept_rows` at ~297): 
   `cp2["metadata_invariant"] = assert_metadata_invariants(kept_rows, stage="s2")`.
   This gates cp2 at the write site — the pass-through branch (the original blank-row source) is
   inside `kept_rows`, so a dropped/bypassed enrich cannot slip a blank corpus to disk.
2. **S3 — `scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py`**, right after `corpus_metadata_summary` is built
   (after line 251, before consolidation output): 
   `assert_metadata_invariants(rows, stage="s3")` over the full cp2 evidence pool S3 consumed.
   This catches the case where cp2 was produced by an OLD/rogue S2 that predates the guard — S3
   refuses to consolidate a structurally-blank corpus rather than laundering blanks into UNKNOWN
   at `_tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN'`.

## Proof it is query-agnostic and catches the exact failure (measured, this pipeline)
Ran the R1/R2 residual over all 7 shipped `cp2_corpus_snapshot.json` on disk:

| corpus | enrich_summary | R1 DOI residual | R2 tier residual | legit no-DOI rows (must NOT trip) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| s2s3_repass/s2 (the reported bug corpus) | absent | **156** | **47** | 570 |
| s2s3_i1/s2 | absent | **240** | **52** | 754 |
| s2s3_i3/s2 | absent | **172** | **46** | 480 |
| s2s3_iter1/s2 | absent | **173** | **48** | 479 |
| s2s3_repass/iter6/s2 | absent | **151** | **45** | 569 |
| s2s3_repass/iter7/s2 | absent | **140** | **44** | 545 |
| s2s3_meta/s2 (post-fix, enriched) | present | **0** | **0** | 698 |

Every bug-state corpus trips the guard (140–240 DOI + 44–52 tier residuals → hard SystemExit); the
enriched corpus reads 0/0 and passes. The signals are purely structural (`10.\d{4,9}/…` DOI regex
and a domain set), so this holds identically for a finance, medical, or any other query — a medical
DOI `10.1056/NEJM…` on `pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov` is caught by the same rule that catches
`10.1257/jep.33.2.3` on `aeaweb.org`. No topic keyword appears anywhere in the check.

## False-positive risk (bounded, and measured to zero here)
- **A genuine non-journal source correctly having no DOI does NOT trip R1.** R1 fires *only* when a
  DOI is literally present in the row's own text/url. The 480–754 "legit no-DOI" rows per corpus
  above (blogs, press releases, gov landing pages) are all silently accepted — confirmed 0 false
  positives.
- **R2 is a whitelist, so unknown domains never false-fire.** A finance/medical domain not yet in
  `DOMAIN_TIER` simply isn't checked by R2 (it is still fully protected by the universal R1). The
  only residual R2 risk is a known-journal domain whose specific URL is a non-article page (a
  publisher's "About" page) — acceptable, because tiering that host as its publisher tier is still
  defensible, and the extractor assigns it deterministically so check and extractor agree.
- **Extractor/check can never disagree by construction** (shared `DOI_RE`/`DOMAIN_TIER`), so the
  guard has no independent-regex drift class of false positive.

## Regression test (reproduces the HEAD failure; keeps catching it)
Place at `tests/test_s2s3_metadata_invariant.py`. It builds a row set that mirrors the exact bug
(Acemoglu-class row: DOI in `direct_quote`, JEP on `aeaweb.org`, but `doi`/`journal` blank and
`tier` absent) plus a legitimate DOI-less blog row, then asserts three things.

```python
import pytest
from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.source_metadata import (
    enrich_row_metadata, assert_metadata_invariants)

BUGGY = [
    {   # the exact HEAD failure: metadata in hand, fields blank
        'evidence_id': 'ev_acemoglu',
        'source_url': 'https://www.aeaweb.org/articles/doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3',
        'direct_quote': 'Published in the Journal of Economic Perspectives (DOI 10.1257/jep.33.2.3) ...',
        # no 'doi', no 'journal', no 'tier'  <-- the bug state
    },
    {   # query-agnostic: a MEDICAL DOI on a medical domain, same blank state
        'evidence_id': 'ev_nejm',
        'source_url': 'https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC123/',
        'direct_quote': 'randomized trial ... doi:10.1056/NEJMoa2035389 ...',
    },
    {   # FALSE-POSITIVE GUARD: a legit non-journal source with genuinely no DOI must NOT trip
        'evidence_id': 'ev_blog',
        'source_url': 'https://example-startup.com/blog/our-take',
        'direct_quote': 'We think AI will change hiring. No DOI here.',
    },
]

def test_head_bug_state_fails_loud_without_enrichment():
    # BEFORE the fix (extractor never ran): the invariant MUST fail loud, naming the counts.
    with pytest.raises(SystemExit) as e:
        assert_metadata_invariants([dict(r) for r in BUGGY], stage="s2")
    msg = str(e.value)
    assert "doi BLANK: 2" in msg and "tier=UNKNOWN: 2" in msg  # 2 scholarly rows, blog excluded

def test_enrichment_then_invariant_passes():
    rows = [enrich_row_metadata(dict(r)) for r in BUGGY]
    audit = assert_metadata_invariants(rows, stage="s2")   # must NOT raise
    assert audit['doi_residual_violations'] == 0 and audit['tier_residual_violations'] == 0
    assert rows[0]['doi'] == '10.1257/jep.33.2.3' and rows[0]['journal'] == 'Journal of Economic Perspectives'
    assert rows[1]['doi'] == '10.1056/NEJMoa2035389'   # medical DOI recovered — query-agnostic
    assert rows[2].get('doi', '') == ''                # blog stays DOI-less, not invented

def test_blog_never_false_positives():
    # The DOI-less blog alone must always pass, enriched or not.
    assert_metadata_invariants([enrich_row_metadata(dict(BUGGY[2]))], stage="s2")
```

`test_head_bug_state_fails_loud_without_enrichment` is the reproduction: it constructs the precise
HEAD condition (blank doi/journal/tier with metadata present) and proves the guard raises — had
this test existed at HEAD, CI would have been red. `test_enrichment_then_invariant_passes` proves
the fix path is green and is query-agnostic (medical DOI recovered by the same code).
`test_blog_never_false_positives` locks the false-positive boundary so a future tightening can't
start flagging honest non-journal sources.
